generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor driving the first vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot, converting electrical energy (input current I_M) to mechanical torque (τ_M) with manufacturer-specified parameters."
]

instance GearWheel : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical gearwheel component in the gear transmission system of the first vertical axis, transmitting rotational energy between motor and cycloidal gearbox."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision cycloidal gearbox in the first vertical axis, providing torque amplification and speed reduction with backlash compensation capabilities."
]

instance Sinumerik840DslController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller enabling separate axis control with position, speed, or acceleration inputs for the MABI MAX 100 robot's first vertical axis."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution position encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor, providing real-time motor output angle (r_M) data."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionData
]

instance SecondaryPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position encoder positioned behind the cycloidal gearbox, providing real-time system output angle (r_out) data for backlash and positional uncertainty reduction."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryPositionData
]

instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring input motor current (I_M) for torque calculation and friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CurrentData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance SecondaryPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryPositionEncoder
]

instance CurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysmlAxisStructuralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) describing the structural composition of the first vertical axis including Motor, GearWheel, and CycloidalGearbox components."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MqttProtocol
]

instance SysmlInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (IBD) defining flow definitions for rotational and current energy flows within the axis system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MqttProtocol
]

instance IdealGearboxSimulinkModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink model simulating ideal gearbox behavior without friction, using manufacturer parameters for dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance CoulombViscousFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink implementation of Coulomb-Viscous friction model applied to the cycloidal gearbox for friction loss analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance StribeckFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink implementation of Stribeck friction model for dynamic friction behavior analysis in the gearbox."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance DahlFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink implementation of Dahl friction model for non-linear friction characteristics in the gear system."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance LugreFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink implementation of LuGre friction model for detailed friction dynamics analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance InputCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed input current data (I_M) derived from CurrentData, used for torque calculations and friction analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CurrentData
]

instance MotorOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed motor output angle data (r_M) derived from MotorPositionData, used for velocity and acceleration calculations."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionData
]

instance SystemOutputData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed system output angle data (r_out) derived from SecondaryPositionData, used for backlash compensation and positional accuracy analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SecondaryPositionData
]

instance TorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived torque data (τ_M, τ_G, τ_out) calculated from input current and manufacturer parameters, used for friction model validation."
    DTDFVocab:fromData InputCurrentData, MotorOutputData, SystemOutputData
]

instance AngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived angular velocity data (ω) calculated from positional data traces, used for motion accuracy analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorOutputData, SystemOutputData
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MqttProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol enabling real-time data streaming from physical sensors to the Digital Shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataStreamingService
]

instance SimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB Simulink environment enabling executable friction models and dynamics simulations for behavioral analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelingService
]

instance SysmlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML tool enabling structural and behavioral modeling of the robot axis components."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelCreationService
]

instance FrictionModelProcessing : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom processing components implementing friction model calculations linked to data traces for traceable computations."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionModelingService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataStreamingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time data streaming service enabling continuous transmission of sensor data from physical system to Digital Shadow."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceabilityInsight
]

instance FrictionModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Friction modeling service continuously identifying static, kinetic, and viscous friction components using white-box and black-box models."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCharacterizationInsight
]

instance SimulationValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Simulation-based validation service comparing simulated data against measured data to detect friction-induced deviations."
    DTDFVocab:provides MotionAccuracyInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integrated visualization service providing unified dashboard for real-time monitoring of torque (τ), angular velocity (ω), and system behavior."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalMonitoringInsight
]

instance ModelCreationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Model creation service developing SysML structural models and Simulink behavioral models for the axis system."
    DTDFVocab:provides AssetDescriptionInsight
]

instance StructuralAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Structural analysis service enabling semantic context linking of data traces to SysML models for traceability."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataTraceabilityInsight
]

instance ControlAlgorithmRefinementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Control algorithm refinement service using data-driven insights to adjust Siemens SINUMERIK controller parameters for enhanced precision."
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterUpdateAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionCharacterizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of static, kinetic, and viscous friction components in the cycloidal gearbox enabling targeted model improvements."
]

instance MotionAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis of deviations between simulated and measured torque (τ) and angular velocity (ω) data traces to assess motion accuracy."
]

instance DataTraceabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Standardized framework for asset description and traceability of data origins through Asset Administration Shells (AAS)."
]

instance AssetDescriptionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structural and behavioral description of the first vertical axis components using SysML models."
]

instance OperationalMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring of system behavior through unified visualization of measured vs. simulated data traces."
]

instance ControlAlgorithmRefinementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Data-driven adjustments to Siemens SINUMERIK controller parameters based on torque/velocity deviation analysis for enhanced precision."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Continuous refinement of friction models leading to updated control parameters for the robot's CNC system."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C12: Environment
instance FirstVerticalAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational environment for the first vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot with angular range [-90°, 90°] and max velocity 75°/s."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, GearWheel, CycloidalGearbox, MotorPositionEncoder, SecondaryPositionEncoder, CurrentSensor, Sinumerik840DslController
]

instance DigitalShadowEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital Shadow environment containing all models, data traces, and processing components for friction analysis."
    base:contains SysmlAxisStructuralModel, SysmlInternalBlockDiagram, IdealGearboxSimulinkModel, CoulombViscousFrictionModel, StribeckFrictionModel, DahlFrictionModel, LugreFrictionModel,
                InputCurrentData, MotorOutputData, SystemOutputData, TorqueData, AngularVelocityData,
                MqttProtocol, SimulinkEnabler, SysmlEnabler, FrictionModelProcessing
]

// Adding missing SystemUnderStudy instance
instance FirstVerticalAxis : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "First vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot focusing on friction modeling for motion accuracy improvement."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment FirstVerticalAxisEnvironment
]

// Adding missing DigitalTwin instance
instance FrictionShadow : DTDFVocab:DigitalTwin [
    base:desc "Digital Twin for friction analysis of the first vertical axis of MABI MAX 100 industrial robot."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment DigitalShadowEnvironment
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time-scale operates in **real-time** with **high-frequency data acquisition** (exact frequency not specified but implied by MQTT streaming and sensor updates). The digital shadow continuously processes **live measurements** (e.g., encoder data, current) and **simulation outputs** to update friction models dynamically. Synchronization between PT and DS occurs at the granularity of **individual data points** (torque/velocity samples) within the operational cycle of the robot axis."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation is structured as a **purpose-driven digital shadow** with the following orchestration:
  1. **Data Sources**: Physical sensors (encoders, current sensors) and derived data (torque/velocity calculations).
  2. **Processing Layer**: Simulation models (Simulink) and data calculation models (friction models) that consume input traces (*I_M*, *r_M*) and produce output traces (*τ̂_G*, *τ̂_out*).
  3. **Model Integration**: SysML structural models (asset description) and simulation models (behavioral analysis) are linked via **traceability associations** to ensure semantic consistency.
  4. **Visualization**: A **unified dashboard** aggregates all data traces (measured vs. simulated) for real-time analysis.
  The system follows a **one-way data flow** (physical → digital) with no feedback to the PT, adhering to the **digital shadow reference model** (DSRM)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The digital twin operates during the **operational phase** of the robot’s life cycle, focusing on **as-operated** representation (real-time monitoring and adaptation). It does not cover design or manufacturing phases but instead **continuously refines friction models** based on runtime data to improve motion accuracy. The DS is **purpose-driven**, targeting **real-time friction detection** and **control optimization** during active use."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through:
  - **Model Validation**: Comparison of **simulated data** (from Simulink) against **measured data** to quantify friction-induced deviations.
  - **Uncertainty Analysis**: Use of **multiple friction models** (white-box and black-box) to cross-validate results and reduce parameter dependency.
  - **Traceability**: Linking data traces to their **sources** (sensors, models) and **processing components** (Simulink blocks) ensures reproducibility.
  - **Verification**: Manufacturer-provided parameters and **sensor calibration** (e.g., encoder accuracy) underpin data fidelity. The DS does not include **closed-loop validation** (no feedback to PT), relying instead on **offline analysis** for model improvement."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical connection between PT and DT involves:
  - **Data Protocols**: **MQTT** for **real-time streaming** of sensor data (*I_M*, encoder readings) from the Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. controller to the DS.
  - **Network Architecture**: **Cyber-physical system (CPS) integration** where sensors act as **data sources** and Simulink models as **processing nodes**.
  - **Data Format**: Time-series data traces stored as **arrays of floating-point values** (torque *τ*, angular velocity *ω*) with **metadata** (source, timestamp, model parameters).
  - **Latency**: Implicitly low-latency due to real-time sensor updates and Simulink’s execution environment."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is hosted in a **software environment** integrating:
  - **SysML Models**: Stored in a **modeling tool** (e.g., IBM Rational Rhapsody or similar) for structural/behavioral description.
  - **Simulation Environment**: MATLAB Simulink for **executable friction models** and dynamics simulations.
  - **Data Storage**: **Time-series databases** (not explicitly named) for storing data traces (*I_M*, *r_M*, etc.).
  - **Visualization Platform**: A **unified dashboard** (likely web-based) for real-time monitoring and analysis. The deployment follows a **hybrid approach**, combining **offline simulation** (for model development) and **online processing** (for live data analysis)."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to the following standards:
  - **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)**: For **structural and behavioral modeling** of the robot axis.
  - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)**: Standardized framework for **asset description** and **data traceability** (per IEC 62422).
  - **MQTT Protocol**: Industry-standard for **real-time data streaming** in IoT/CPS applications.
  - **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)**: Methodologies like **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)** for system abstraction.
  The DS follows the **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)** as defined in [15], ensuring interoperability with other MBSE-compliant tools."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety considerations include:
  - **Data Integrity**: MQTT ensures **encrypted data transmission** (implied by industrial IoT standards) to prevent tampering.
  - **Access Control**: The DS is designed as a **passive entity** (no autonomous actions), reducing risks of unauthorized PT manipulation.
  - **Safety Cell Integration**: The robot operates within a **restricted workspace** ([−90°, 90°]) and **speed limits** (75°/s), mitigating collision risks.
  - **No Remote Operation**: The DS does not execute **remote commands** on the PT, adhering to the **digital shadow’s one-way data flow** principle. Safety is ensured by the **CNC controller’s closed-loop control** (Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl.), which remains the authoritative system for motion execution."
]
